_meta.name: C4 F1-TC bf16 WMMA prep — reconnaissance + launch plan
_meta.description: Recon-only reconnaissance of C2 struct-args ABI status + F1-TC bf16 Tensor Core implementation plan. No code written, no commits. Produced after the C4 F1 fp32 MVP bench (shared/state/c4_f1_fused_qkv_bench_20260417.json) showed GATE_PARTIAL — correctness PASS, fp32-only performance hits compute-bound at production shapes. The next lever is bf16 WMMA behind c2_struct_args. This file maps where that ABI lives and how to wire F1-TC once the audit confirms readiness.
_meta.constraint: recon only — NO code edits, NO .py, NO H100 launch, NO commits

c2_struct_args_abi_status.verdict: LIVE — not a blocker. Design doc's `c2_struct_args ABI blocker` note is obsolete as of hexa-lang commit 3760238 (C2 Step 3, 2026-04-16T00:10Z KST).

evidence_primary.hexa_lang_commit_title: feat(rt+interp+codegen): C2 Step 3 interp FFI + C3 PBV in-place + C1 native-int for — Day-1 runtime uplift
hexa_lang_files_landed: /Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/self/runtime.c :: hexa_host_ffi_call_14 (14-arg FFI dispatch, scalar fanout, float_mask, ret_kind)
/Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/self/runtime.c :: hexa_ptr_alloc + hexa_ptr_write + hexa_ptr_free (raw struct-arg buffer ABI)
/Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/self/ml/hxcuda.hexa :: `extern fn hxcuda_matmul_bf16(args_p: *Void) -> int` + HXCUDA_MM_STRUCT_BYTES=48 + HXCUDA_LM_STRUCT_BYTES=80 offset tables

/Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/self/native/hxcuda_fused.cu :: hxcuda_matmul_bf16_kernel with wmma::fragment<matrix_a/b/accumulator, 16,16,16, __nv_bfloat16, row_major> + fp32 accumulator + __float2bfloat16 staging
evidence_primary.bench_pass: bench(C2): hxlayer FFI DIM sweep 64..1024 — 12.5x→50x interpreter vs FFI (hexa-lang commit 1f5c9c1)

evidence_secondary_anima_already_uses_it.pattern: extern fn hxlmhead_bwd(args_p: *Void) -> int with HXLMH_STRUCT_BYTES=120 using `alloc_raw(120) + ptr_write(off, S/H/M/V/loss_scale/x/mid/targets/Wu/Wv/dL_dx/dW_u/dW_v/loss_out/reserved0)`
evidence_secondary_anima_already_uses_it.note: Anima CLM already production-uses the exact struct-args ABI that F1-TC needs — there is no new ABI integration cost.

status_in_c_track_board.actual_status: Step 1 + Step 3 LANDED (rmsnorm+silu fused + 14-arg scalar FFI + struct-args ABI). Step 2 (bulk per-layer hxlayer_llama_forward) remains in progress but is NOT required for F1-TC.
status_in_c_track_board.correction_recommended: C2 Step 3 landing on 2026-04-16 already supplies everything F1-TC needs — board is stale by 1 day.

remaining_c2_work_orthogonal_to_f1_tc: C2 Step 2 full-layer bulk FFI (hxlayer_llama_forward) — this is the 20-field struct that spans every per-layer weight pointer. Not needed for F1 (QKV projection only).
C10 H100 wire (grad ckpt GPU path) — depends on Step 2, not on F1-TC.

f1_tc_bf16_wmma_implementation_plan.scope: Upgrade training/c4_fused_kernels.hexa :: K_C4_QKV_PROJ_FUSED_FWD NVRTC kernel string from fp32 per-thread scalar reduction to bf16 WMMA 16x16x16 Tensor Core tile.
f1_tc_bf16_wmma_implementation_plan.chosen_path: NVRTC string (in-file kernel, rtc_launch) — NOT FFI @link. The C4 MVP already uses rtc_compile_ptx → rtc_get_function → rtc_launch with an arg-pointer array (_c4_arg_ptr/_c4_arg_i32). F1-TC keeps this path because (a) no new dylib to build on H100 pod, (b) no ABI coupling with hexa-lang repo, (c) NVRTC strings rebuild per-run so iterating is 2 lines of string edit. The struct-args ABI is used only by `@link`-imported C functions; rtc_launch-driven NVRTC kernels pass args as a pointer-array-of-pointers, which the MVP already supports.
f1_tc_bf16_wmma_implementation_plan.alternative_path_rejected: Port QKV fusion into libhxcuda.so with `@link('hxcuda')` — rejected because it adds a build dependency (nvcc on H100 pod, hexa-lang dylib install) and cross-repo coupling, for zero functional benefit over the in-file NVRTC path.

change_points.change: ADD `#include <cuda_fp16.h>` + `#include <mma.h>` + `using namespace nvcuda;` at top of NVRTC string — NVRTC 11.2 on pod supports both, no extra flags needed.

KEEP `const float* norm, const float* Wqkv, float* outQ/K/V, int M, int K_in, int DhH`. Inputs/outputs stay fp32 — matches design doc §4.2 'fp32 in / fp32 out, bf16 internal' to avoid disrupting hexa-side weight storage.

CHANGE from block(16,16,1) grid(ceil(M/16),ceil(N/16),1) = 256 threads/block, 1 thread per output → block(32,1,1) grid(ceil(N/16),ceil(M/16),1) = 1 warp/block, 1 warp per 16x16 output tile. Matches hxcuda_matmul_bf16_kernel shape exactly.

ADD `wmma::fragment<matrix_a, 16,16,16, __nv_bfloat16, row_major> a_frag; wmma::fragment<matrix_b, 16,16,16, __nv_bfloat16, row_major> b_frag; wmma::fragment<accumulator, 16,16,16, float> c_frag; wmma::fill_fragment(c_frag, 0.0f);`
ADD `__shared__ __nv_bfloat16 smem_a[16*16]; __shared__ __nv_bfloat16 smem_b[16*16];` (512 bytes/block, well under 228KB cap)

REPLACE `for (k=0; k<K_in; k++) acc += norm[m*K_in+k] * Wqkv[k*N+n]` (per-thread scalar) with `for (k_step=0; k_step<K_in; k_step+=16) { cooperatively-load smem_a/b with __float2bfloat16; __syncthreads(); wmma::load_matrix_sync(a_frag,smem_a,16); wmma::load_matrix_sync(b_frag,smem_b,16); wmma::mma_sync(c_frag,a_frag,b_frag,c_frag); __syncthreads(); }`

REPLACE per-thread `if (slot==0) outQ[idx]=acc; else if (slot==1) outK[idx]=acc; else outV[idx]=acc;` with `wmma::store_matrix_sync` into a temp fp32 16x16 tile, then let lane 0..31 dispatch to outQ/outK/outV based on warpN/DhH. The slot-split happens per-warp not per-thread — same slot math, applied to the tile's base column.
KEEP `if (m>=M || n>=N) return` guard semantics; replace with `if (warpM<M && warpN<N)` guarding the wmma::store_matrix_sync call (matches hxcuda_matmul_bf16_kernel:136-138).

launcher_changes_c4_impl_qkv_proj_fwd.bx_by_change: CHANGE from bx=16, by=16 (256 threads/block) to bx=32, by=1 (1 warp/block). gx/gy swap roles: gx=ceil(N/16), gy=ceil(M/16).
launcher_changes_c4_impl_qkv_proj_fwd.args_unchanged: The _c4_arg_ptr/_c4_arg_i32 arg-pointer array packed for rtc_launch stays byte-identical — NVRTC WMMA kernels accept the same uintptr_t scalar-pointer dispatch.
launcher_changes_c4_impl_qkv_proj_fwd.rtc_compile_flags: ADD `-arch=sm_86` (RTX 3090) or `-arch=sm_90` (H100) at rtc_compile_ptx call site. Current MVP uses `compute_86 -default-device`. For H100 switch to `-arch=sm_90 -use_fast_math -DNDEBUG` per design doc §9.
launcher_changes_c4_impl_qkv_proj_fwd.initialization: No change — c4_init_modules() still compiles a single NVRTC module holding the kernel string.

numerical_tolerance_change.bf16_target_tol: 1e-2 relative (bf16 mantissa is 8 bits — 1/128 per-multiply relative error, K=1024 accumulation → ~1e-2 ceiling). Matches design doc §6.1 'WMMA bf16 fused vs pure-hexa ref: ≤ 1e-2 relative'.
numerical_tolerance_change.gate_numerical: max |fused_bf16 - fused_fp32| / max |fused_fp32| < 1e-2 on S3 shape (M=4096, K_in=1024, D_head=64, H=16, DhH=1024).

expected_performance_lift.forecast_source: docs/c4-cuda-fused-megakernel-design.md §3 (F1 '2.5× (1.4× launch + 1.8× TC / input reuse)') — fp32 MVP captured ~1.0x of that at CLM 1B shape (launch reduction only at large shapes); TC captures the remaining 1.8x multiplicative.
expected_performance_lift.cumulative_vs_unfused_fp32_baseline: 2.5x target (fp32 mvp 1.0x × TC 1.8x = ~1.8x baseline; design doc also counts input-reuse fragment caching as part of the 2.5x)
expected_performance_lift.mfu_projection_h100_clm_1b: 27% → ~49% from F1-TC alone (design doc §3.1 combined with F2+F3 targets 55-65%)

previously_believed_blocker.description: c2_struct_args ABI not yet landed — F1-TC deferred until hexa-lang exposes the struct-args FFI
previously_believed_blocker.actual_status: CLEARED on 2026-04-16 via hexa-lang commit 3760238 (3 days ago relative to 2026-04-17)
previously_believed_blocker.resolution_path: No action required — F1-TC can proceed immediately against the in-file NVRTC path, which doesn't even need the struct-args ABI (it uses rtc_launch's arg-pointer array).

soft_blockers_not_on_critical_path.description: F1-TC bf16 WMMA requires sm_80+ (A100) or sm_90 (H100). RTX 3090 is sm_86 — WMMA bf16 works on sm_86 per CUDA 11.2+ (introduced in sm_80). Bench pod 64.228.13.219 is RTX 3090 = sm_86 → F1-TC bench will succeed on that pod but with ~1/3 the Tensor Core TFLOPS of H100. For the final MFU gate measurement we still need an H100.
soft_blockers_not_on_critical_path.impact: informational — does NOT block bring-up; only the final gate measurement needs H100.
soft_blockers_not_on_critical_path.mitigation: Bench on RTX 3090 for correctness + ratio, promote to H100 for MFU numbers.

soft_blockers_not_on_critical_path.description: Design doc §5.3 says stay unfused when d < 256 (WMMA 16x16 tile granularity wastes TC area at tiny d). F1-TC must add the shape gate in c4_impl_qkv_proj_fwd.

integration_eta_if_approved_now.nvrtc_string_edit: ~40 min (copy structure from hxcuda_matmul_bf16_kernel, replace scalar K-loop with WMMA K-loop, keep slot-split store)
integration_eta_if_approved_now.launcher_edit: ~10 min (bx=32 by=1, flip grid dims, add -arch=sm_XX flag passthrough, add shape gate)
integration_eta_if_approved_now.rtx_3090_correctness_bench: ~30 min (compile + run against existing c4_gpu_bench.c harness, compare against c4_ref_qkv_unfused tolerance 1e-2)
integration_eta_if_approved_now.h100_correctness_and_mfu_bench: ~60 min (H100 pod already available to project; rerun same harness with nvidia-smi dmon -s u capture)
integration_eta_if_approved_now.total: ~140 min wall-clock from approval to H100 MFU number

ready_now: hxcuda_fused.cu WMMA bf16 reference kernel — LANDED (hexa-lang/self/native/hxcuda_fused.cu:70-139)
anima NVRTC path — LIVE (training/c4_fused_kernels.hexa §3, rtc_init + rtc_compile_ptx + rtc_launch proven on RTX 3090 pod 64.228.13.219:61320)
CPU reference oracle — LIVE (c4_ref_qkv_unfused + c4_ref_qkv_fused, used by bench harness)
Bench harness — LIVE (c4_gpu_bench.c compiled with -lnvrtc -lcuda, 5 shapes validated)
Design doc guidance — LIVE (docs/c4-cuda-fused-megakernel-design.md §4.1 chooses m16n16k16, §4.2 fp32 in/fp32 out/bf16 internal, §4.3 shmem budget 1536B)
Board item ownership — anima_session owns C4 unambiguously
required_before_launch: User approval to write code (this session is recon-only — must be greenlit before editing c4_fused_kernels.hexa)
Confirm C-track audit session (the 'other session' holding uncommitted work) is not simultaneously editing c4_fused_kernels.hexa — grep git status on anima repo before writing
RTX 3090 pod 64.228.13.219:61320 still warm (last-known status 'idle_at_start' per bench file; low risk)
launch_sequence: Step 1: Edit training/c4_fused_kernels.hexa § K_C4_QKV_PROJ_FUSED_FWD string → bf16 WMMA variant per plan above
Step 2: Edit c4_impl_qkv_proj_fwd launcher bx/by flip + shape gate
Step 3: Add -arch=sm_86 to rtc_compile_ptx call site (parameterize via env var CUDA_ARCH for H100 later)
Step 4: Rebuild /tmp/c4_gpu_bench on RTX 3090 pod, run against same 5 shapes
Step 5: Expected result — S1 small ≥ 1.0x (WMMA wasteful at K=256), S3 design-doc shape ≥ 1.5x (TC kicks in at K=1024), bit-exact tolerance 1e-2
Step 6: If RTX 3090 PASS, dispatch agent to H100 pod for MFU measurement against nvidia-smi dmon -s u
Step 7: Write shared/state/c4_f1_tc_bf16_bench_YYYYMMDD.json with before/after speedup + MFU number
Step 8: Update training_speed_ceilings.json :: CLM with the F1-TC gain
Step 9: Flag C4 item status → 'f1_tc_fp32_bf16_done_pending_f2_f3' in c_track_board.json

Verified via `git status` on /Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang — changes confirmed uncommitted, all in hexa-lang repo (not anima). No conflict with F1-TC work on anima/training/c4_fused_kernels.hexa.
cross_session_coordination_notes.anima_repo_clean_for_c4_edits: Anima repo tip is 6184d668 (7B pre-flight regression 40/40 + C2 F1 fused QKV MVP). No uncommitted changes to training/c4_fused_kernels.hexa detected from task briefing context.
cross_session_coordination_notes.recommended_coordination: Before writing F1-TC code, post a claim to c_track_board.json :: items.C4 flipping status 'pre_claimed_pending_c2_landing' → 'claimed_in_progress' with session_id of executing agent. Post an entry to session_log.

next_action_recommendation: Get user approval to promote this recon into implementation. The bottleneck said to be c2_struct_args ABI is in fact resolved — F1-TC can launch immediately. Expected wall-clock from green-light to H100 MFU number: ~140 minutes.
